title: Nonuniqueness of Leray-Hopf solutions to the unforced incompressible 3D Navier-Stokes Equation

thm:1.summary: There exist infinitely many distinct suitable Leray-Hopf solutions to the unforced NSE on $R^3\times[0,1]$ with the same initial data $u_in\inL^q (q<3)$, living in $L^s L^q$ spaces that just miss the Prodi-Serrin uniqueness threshold (3/q+2/s>1).

def:1.summary: Leray-Hopf solution: divergence-free vector field in $L\infty_loc L^2\capL^2_loc H^1$ satisfying the weak NSE formulation and the global energy inequality.

rem:1.summary: Suitability: Leray's construction yields the stronger local energy inequality (1.2), enabling partial regularity via Caffarelli-Kohn-Nirenberg theory.

rem:2.summary: The self-similar 1/|x| profile is not in $L^2$, but Jia-Sverak showed it can be localized to yield valid Leray-Hopf initial data $u_in$.

thm:2.summary: Existence of a weak solution (≈®, αΒΦ, $\lambda$ΧÉ) with ≈®$\inH^1_loc$ and αΒΦ$\inH^1$ to the coupled self-similar NSE + linearized eigenvalue system (1.12); the negative eigenvalue $\lambda$ΧÉ<0 certifies an unstable perturbation yielding the second Leray-Hopf solution.

assump:1.summary: Approximate profiles (≈Σ, PΧ³, vΧ³, qΧ³, $\lambda$Χ³) nearly solving the coupled NSE+eigenvalue system (1.12) with residuals bounded by $\varepsilon^U$ and $\varepsilon^v$, satisfying $1/|\xi|$ far-field decay (1.13)-(1.14). Verified numerically.

prop:1.summary: Given Assump 1, there exists an exact perturbation $U\inH^1$ with βÄ•UβÄ•$_{L^2}$βâ≤$\varepsilon^U$ such that ≈®=≈Σ+U solves the NSE component of (1.12). Proved by applying the abstract Thm 3.

prop:2.summary: Given Assump 1 and Prop 1, there exists an exact perturbation $(\lambda,v)\inH^1$ such that $(\lambda$Χ³$+\lambda$, vΧ³+v) is a genuine eigenpair of the linearized operator with negative eigenvalue, certifying an unstable direction. Proved by applying Thm 3.

prop:3.summary: The exact self-similar profile ≈® from Prop 1 is smooth $(C\infty$β²¦ away from origin).

prop:4.summary: ≈® from Prop 1 satisfies the same far-field boundary condition (1.7) as the approximation ≈Σ, ensuring the self-similar setup is self-consistent.

assump:2.summary: Abstract framework: Hilbert chain $H^1\subsetB\subsetH^0$ with compact embedding $H^1\toB$, and operator decomposition L=Lβ²¹+Lβ²² with Lβ²¹ coercive (constant Kβ²¹) and Lβ²² compact/bounded (constant Kβ²²).

assump:3.summary: Finite-rank operator $I_n$ approximates identity with interpolation error $\delta$β²¹(n)βÄ•fβÄ•$_{H^1}$; solution $\Phi$ to the projected system (3.11) exists with small residual $\varepsilon$β²¹ and explicit $H^1$ bound $\gamma$β²²(n).

assump:4.summary: Residual E is sufficiently small: βÄ•EβÄ•$_{H^0}\le\varepsilon$β²². This is the numerically-verified condition (via computer-assisted bounds) that closes the proof.

assump:5.summary: Nonlinear term N(w) satisfies quadratic boundedness (Kβ²É bound in $H^1)$ and Lipschitz continuity in B-norm, enabling a Schauder fixed-point argument.

thm:3.summary: Abstract existence: if Assump 2-5 hold and the discriminant conditions (3.16)-(3.17) on Kβ²¹,Kβ²²,$\delta$β²¹,$\varepsilon$β²¹,$\varepsilon$β²²,Kβ²É are satisfied, then (3.13) has a solution wβ²¹ with explicit $H^1$ bound (3.18). Core engine of the computer-assisted proof.

rem:3.summary: Quadratic nonlinearity with Sobolev embedding satisfies Assump 5; proof uses Schauder fixed-point on a ball in $H^1$.

rem:4.summary: Logical order for verifying Thm 3: first check Assump 2,5 qualitatively; choose n large for Assump 3; choose $\varepsilon$β²² small for Assump 4.

rem:5.summary: Key implementation shortcut: rather than verifying all five assumptions separately, verify Assump 2,4 and directly extract estimates (3.20),(3.24) from the Thm 3 proof for sharper constants.

lem:1.summary: Quasi-orthonormal projection: estimate (4.13) controls how close the Q-orthogonal projection onto $V_lg$ is to exact Q-orthonormality via Gram matrix constant $\varepsilon$β²É (eq. 4.14).

lem:2.summary: Quasi-orthogonality: for $f\inV_lg$ and u Q-orthogonal to $V_lg, |$βü®f,uβü©$_E|\le\varepsilon$β²³βÄ•fβÄ•_EβÄ•uβÄ•_E, where $\varepsilon$β²³ measures residuals of the approximate eigenvector problem.

lem:3.summary: Finite-rank approximation bound: |βü®(Lβ²²$-L_ap)f,f$βü©$|\leM$β²¹βÄ•$\nablaf$βÄ•^2+Mβ²²βÄ•fβÄ•^2, where Mβ²¹,Mβ²² depend on $\varepsilon$β²É,$\varepsilon$β²³ from Lem 1-2 and spectral constants Kβ²Ö,Kβ²Ü,Kβ²΅.

assump:6.summary: Bounded mapping assumption for U: discriminant condition Mβ²³^U>0, ensuring the nonlinear iteration for the self-similar profile closes with an $H^1$ bound.

assump:7.summary: Bounded mapping assumption for v: discriminant condition Mβ²³^v>0 incorporating eigenvalue correction terms and bounds on U from Prop 1, ensuring the eigenfunction iteration closes.

rem:6.summary: Dirichlet BC at finite radius R distorts numerical solutions (Guillod-Sverak issue); interpolation-based extension is used instead for rigorous analysis.

rem:7.summary: Numerical instabilities produce spurious high-oscillation eigenvalues, but the method still accurately captures genuine eigenmodes of the linearized operator.

lem:4.summary: Complete characterization of Stokes eigenpairs on the 3D unit ball: first is $(0,(cos\theta,-sin\theta,0))$; remaining indexed by two families of Bessel zeros via vector spherical harmonics (Y,Z families, eqs 6.3-6.4).

rem:8.summary: Rescaling for $\Omega=B_0(32)$: eigenvalues scale as $\lambda^s_n/R$β²Ä^2; retain only even or odd-l harmonics based on parity of U and v.

lem:5.summary: Distribution of Bessel zeros: equations (6.3) and (6.4) each have exactly one root per interlacing interval; first zeros satisfy lower bounds $\alpha^Y_{l,1}\ge\sqrts_l$ and $\alpha^Z_{l,1}\get_l=\sqrt(l(l+1))$.

rem:9.summary: Boundary condition for $\varphi^U_n$ requires homogeneous Dirichlet $(sin(2m\beta)$ basis), differing from the general-U condition $sin((2m-1)\beta)$; FEM must impose this exactly.

lem:6.summary: Sobolev embedding in 3D: βÄ•uβÄ•$^2_{L^4}\leK$β²³(βÄ•uβÄ•$_{L^2}+$βÄ•$\nablau$βÄ•$_{L^2})^2$ with explicit constant Kβ²³$=3^{3/4}/(8\pi)\approx0.0907$, used to control the NSE nonlinearity.

rem:10.summary: Recurrence identity $J'_{l+1/2}(r)=J_{l-1/2}(r)-(l+1/2)/r\cdotJ_{l+1/2}(r)$ simplifies eqs (6.3)-(6.4) for numerical root-finding.

edges.note: Nonuniqueness follows from Thm 2: negative eigenvalue $\lambda$ΧÉ<0 implies existence of a second Leray-Hopf solution via the self-similar perturbation
Thm 2 combines Props 1-2; Prop 1 provides the base exact self-similar solution ≈®
Prop 2 provides the exact unstable eigenpair $(\lambda$ΧÉ,αΒΦ) certifying instability and the second solution
Prop 1 begins 'If Assumption 1 holds...' βÄî numerically verified approximate profiles are the input
Prop 2 begins 'If Assumption 1 holds...'
Prop 2 uses ≈®=≈Σ+U from Prop 1 in the linearized eigenvalue equation (1.17)
Smoothness is a property of ≈® constructed in Prop 1
Far-field asymptotics are a property of ≈® from Prop 1
Prop 1 is proved by instantiating abstract Thm 3 for the U-equation (1.15)
Prop 2 is proved by instantiating abstract Thm 3 for the v-equation (1.17)
Thm 3 requires coercive+compact decomposition of L (Assump 2)
Thm 3 requires finite-rank approximation with small $\delta$β²¹(n) (Assump 3)
Thm 3 requires small residual $\varepsilon$β²² (Assump 4)
Thm 3 requires quadratic nonlinear estimates (Assump 5)
Bounded mapping condition Mβ²³^U>0 (Assump 6) is verified to apply Thm 3 to the U-equation
Bounded mapping condition Mβ²³^v>0 (Assump 7) is verified to apply Thm 3 to the v-equation
Assump 7 references βÄ•UβÄ•$_{L^2}$ and βÄ•$\nablaU$βÄ•$_{L^2}$ bounds from Prop 1 to define $x_0^U, x_1^U$
Lem 3 uses quasi-orthonormality constant $\varepsilon$β²É from Lem 1 in the bound for Mβ²²
Lem 3 uses quasi-orthogonality constant $\varepsilon$β²³ from Lem 2 in the bound for Mβ²¹
Assump 3 is instantiated using Lem 3 to bound the finite-rank approximation error
Mβ²¹,Mβ²² from Lem 3 enter the discriminant Mβ²³^U in Assump 6
Mβ²¹,Mβ²² from Lem 3 enter the discriminant Mβ²³^v in Assump 7
Lem 4 uses the zero distribution from Lem 5 to verify completeness of the Bessel-based eigenfunction expansion
Sobolev $L^4$ embedding (Lem 6, explicit Kβ²³) used to verify Assump 5 for the NSE nonlinearity $u\cdot\nablau$
Definition of Leray-Hopf frames the precise meaning of 'nonuniqueness' in Thm 1
Rem 5 describes how to bypass the full assumption list and extract sharper constants directly from the Thm 3 proof

Definitions_and_Setup.description: Foundational definitions establishing Leray-Hopf solutions and the self-similar initial data setting

Main_Result_and_Reduction.description: The main nonuniqueness theorem and its immediate reduction to constructing an exact solution-plus-negative-eigenvalue pair

Self_Similar_Input_Profiles.description: Numerically-verified approximate profiles that serve as input to the computer-assisted existence argument

Exact_Solution_Construction.description: Core propositions: exact self-similar profile and exact unstable eigenpair, both obtained by perturbing numerical approximations

Abstract_Existence_Framework.description: Abstract Hilbert space framework (coercive+compact, Schauder fixed-point) enabling rigorous computer-assisted verification of existence

Functional_Analysis_Tools.description: Quasi-orthogonality lemmas, finite-rank bounds, Sobolev embedding, and bounded-mapping assumptions that instantiate the abstract framework

Numerical_Basis_Construction.description: Explicit Stokes eigenbasis on the ball via Bessel functions, including scaling, parity selection, and boundary conditions for the FEM solver
